hero.title: 5 Days in North Vietnam.
hero.titleAccent: Ancient Hanoi, Sapa's Terraced Highlands & Vietnam's Highest Peak.
hero.intro: Two completely different Vietnams in five days. Ancient Hanoi: thousand-year-old streets, egg coffee, and Cha Ca grilled fish. Then Sapa — the highland plateau where the Hoang Lien Son range rises to 3,147 metres and H'Mong communities have farmed impossibly steep terraced slopes for two centuries. And above it all: Fansipan, the Roof of Indochina, reached by the world's longest three-rope cable car from Sapa town.
hero.heroImageAlt: Sapa terraced rice fields H'mong village North Vietnam Fansipan highlands

quickFacts.saving: Save RM472 per person (20% online savings)

Fansipan Sun World combo (Muong Hoa train + cable car + funicular + buffet lunch)
Daily breakfast, lunches and dinners as specified
excludes: Rainbow Slide at Cat Cat Village (own expense)

overview.heading: 5 Days North Vietnam: Hanoi, Sapa & Fansipan
body: This five-day itinerary covers the two faces of North Vietnam that most travellers want to experience: Hanoi's ancient urban culture and the highland landscape of Sapa — then adds Fansipan, Vietnam's highest peak at 3,147 metres, reached by the Sun World cable car system from Sapa town. It is the most focused North Vietnam highland package, with two nights in Sapa giving genuine time to explore the mountain landscape.

The drive from Hanoi to Sapa (06:30 AM pickup, 320 km, approximately 5.5 hours via the Lao Cai expressway) is the standard and most efficient route — a comfortable private van journey through the Red River Delta farmland and into the Hoang Lien Son mountain range, arriving in time for lunch and an afternoon at Cat Cat Village. The expressway has reduced the Hanoi-Sapa drive significantly from the pre-highway era.

The Fansipan experience on day three is the package's centrepiece: the Muong Hoa Hiking Train from Sun Plaza Station in Sapa town to the cable car boarding station, the gondola ascent to 3,143 metres, the funicular to the peak, and the 11 Buddhist spiritual sites that have been built into the mountain's upper slopes — including the 21.5-metre Great Buddha (50 tons of copper) and the 12-metre Guan Yin statue (18 tons). The full Fansipan combo (train + cable + funicular + all entrance fees + buffet lunch) is included.

highlights.heading: Why This 5-Day Hanoi–Sapa Package Stands Apart

items.title: Fansipan — Roof of Indochina by Cable Car
items.body: At 3,147 metres, Fansipan is the highest peak in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia combined — the Roof of Indochina. The Sun World Fansipan Legend system from Sapa town includes the Muong Hoa Hiking Train (a mountain rack railway from Sun Plaza to the cable car boarding station) and the gondola cable car (6,282 metres total, the world's longest three-rope cable car) to the summit at 3,143 metres. The full Fansipan combo (train + cable + funicular + 11 Buddhist sites + Great Buddha + Guan Yin + buffet lunch) is included in the package price.
items.imageAlt: Fansipan peak cable car Great Buddha Sapa North Vietnam

items.title: Cat Cat Village — Black H'Mong Culture
items.body: Cat Cat Village is the closest Black H'Mong community to Sapa town — a 30-minute walk downhill from the town centre through rice paddy terraces, past water buffaloes grazing on steep slopes, to a waterfall and village where the H'Mong community has lived for generations. Women at the village entrance sell hand-dyed indigo fabrics and silver accessories made by traditional techniques. The Rainbow Slide — a downhill slide track through the village — is optional at own expense (approximately RM10). The view from the terraces above Cat Cat extends across the full Muong Hoa Valley.
items.imageAlt: Cat Cat Village Black H'Mong Sapa North Vietnam rice terraces

items.title: Sapa Highland Landscape
items.body: The Muong Hoa Valley below Sapa town contains some of the most photographed terraced landscapes in Asia — rice paddies carved into near-vertical slopes by H'Mong, Red Dao, Tay, and Giay communities over two centuries. Sapa town itself sits at 1,500 metres elevation in a natural amphitheatre, with the Hoang Lien Son range rising behind it. The morning mist that fills the valley before 09:00 and the cloud that wraps the higher ridges in the afternoon are the defining atmospheric qualities of the highland landscape. The terraces are most spectacular September–October (golden harvest) and May–June (water-filled reflection season).
items.imageAlt: Sapa Muong Hoa Valley terraced rice paddies highland Vietnam

items.title: Hanoi City Tour — 1,000 Years of History
items.body: Hanoi's essential imperial and cultural sights cover a thousand years of Vietnamese history: Tran Quoc Pagoda (6th-century Buddhist pagoda, the oldest in Hanoi), the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum on Ba Dinh Square (where Ho Chi Minh declared independence in 1945), Hoan Kiem Lake (the Returned Sword Lake), the Hanoi Opera House (1901 French Beaux Arts), and the 36-guild Old Quarter — each street named for the trade it once hosted exclusively. The Cha Ca dinner introduces Hanoi's most distinctive dish: turmeric-marinated fish cooked at the table with fresh dill.
items.imageAlt: Hanoi city tour Hoan Kiem Lake Old Quarter Vietnam

days.title: Arrive Hanoi — City Tour
days.description: Arrive at Noi Bai International Airport; hotel check-in. Full Hanoi city tour: Tran Quoc Pagoda, Ba Dinh Square, Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, Hoan Kiem Lake, St Joseph's Cathedral, Hanoi Opera House, Old Quarter (36 Ancient Streets). En route: pho tasting and traditional Hanoi egg coffee. Evening dinner at a Cha Ca grilled fish restaurant — Hanoi's signature dish of turmeric-marinated fish cooked at the table with dill and spring onions.
suggestedActivities: Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum and Ba Dinh Square
Hoan Kiem Lake and Ngoc Son Temple

days.title: Drive to Sapa — Cat Cat Village
days.description: 06:30–06:45: Driver picks up guests from hotel for the Sapa drive (320 km via Lao Cai expressway, approximately 5.5 hours with comfort stops). Arrive Sapa midday. Hotel check-in and lunch at local restaurant. Afternoon: guided walk to Cat Cat Village — 30 minutes downhill through terraced rice paddies to the Black H'Mong settlement at the valley base. Village waterfall, traditional indigo weaving, and handicraft stalls. Optional Rainbow Slide (own expense, approximately RM10). Evening: return to hotel for dinner.
suggestedActivities: Cat Cat Village walk (30 min downhill)

days.title: Fansipan Cable Car & Sun World
days.description: 08:00: Meet guide at hotel; walk to Sun Plaza Station in Sapa town. Board Muong Hoa Hiking Train to Sun World Fansipan Legend Station. Full Fansipan combo included: cable car ascent to 3,143m, funicular to the peak, all entrance fees. At the summit: 11 Buddhist spiritual sites including the Great Buddha (21.5m, 50 tons of copper) and Guan Yin statue (12m, 18 tons). On clear days, views across Hoang Lien Son National Park into Yunnan, China. Buffet lunch at Van Sam Restaurant (Vietnamese, Chinese, and international dishes). Allow 4+ hours at the summit complex. Afternoon: Moana Sapa for highland viewpoints (Infinity Swing, Bali Gate, Golden Hand sculpture). Hotel dinner at own arrangement.
suggestedActivities: Muong Hoa Hiking Train from Sun Plaza
Buffet lunch at Van Sam Restaurant
Moana Sapa evening (Infinity Swing, Bali Gate)

days.title: Sapa Town Walk — Return to Hanoi
days.description: Free morning in Sapa for a leisurely town walk: Sapa main square, the Catholic Church of Our Lady of the Rosary (1926 stone church), Sapa Culture Museum (H'Mong, Dao, and Giay ethnic minority exhibits), and the Sapa Old Market. Coffee at Cong Caphe — the Communist-era inspired Vietnamese café chain, with panoramic Muong Hoa Valley views from the terrace. Check out by noon; return drive to Hanoi (approximately 5 hours). Dinner at own arrangement.
suggestedActivities: Sapa town square and bell tower
Catholic Church of Our Lady of the Rosary
Cong Caphe coffee with valley views
Noon checkout and return drive to Hanoi

days.title: Hanoi Free Morning — Depart
days.description: Free morning in Hanoi. Optional: Temple of Literature (Vietnam's first university, 1070), Hoa Lo Prison Museum (the Hanoi Hilton), or a final Old Quarter walk for banh mi and egg coffee. Transfer to Noi Bai International Airport per agreed schedule.
suggestedActivities: Optional: Old Quarter banh mi and egg coffee

whyBook.heading: Vietnam's Highlands — Arranged by Malaysia's Trusted Agency Since 1988
whyBook.body: Sapa is a destination where the quality of your guide matters enormously. The landscape is vast, the hill tribe communities are accessible only with local knowledge, and the Fansipan cable car experience requires correct timing. Dhesu's Sapa guides know when the terraces are most photogenic, how to navigate the Fansipan complex efficiently, and where to find the best Sapa highland cuisine. We have been arranging highland Vietnam journeys for Malaysian travellers since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. Sapa is one of our speciality North Vietnam destinations — we have arranged hundreds of highland tours.

points.title: 35+ Years of Vietnam Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been sending Malaysian travellers through Vietnam's highlands. Our Sapa guides are trusted long-term partners with genuine local community connections.

points.title: Full Fansipan Combo Included
points.body: The complete Sun World Fansipan combo (Muong Hoa train + cable car + funicular + all entrance fees + buffet lunch) is included. No separate ticketing at the mountain.

points.title: Buy 3 Free 1 Promotion
points.body: Groups of 4 pay for 3 — the 4th person travels free. Ask about this offer when booking.

sections.heading: 5 Day Hanoi Sapa Fansipan Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
The 5-day Hanoi, Sapa, and Fansipan tour is the most focused North Vietnam highland itinerary for Malaysian travellers. Two nights in Sapa allows both the Cat Cat Village walk and the full Fansipan Sun World cable car experience without rushing. The drive from Hanoi to Sapa via the Lao Cai expressway is approximately 5.5 hours by private vehicle — comfortable and efficient, with the highland scenery beginning after the first 3 hours of expressway.

Fansipan at 3,147 metres is the highest peak in Indochina — higher than anything in Malaysia, Thailand, or Cambodia. The Sun World Fansipan Legend complex, including the 6,282-metre three-rope cable car (the world's longest), makes the summit accessible to all travellers regardless of fitness level. The Great Buddha statue (21.5 metres, 50 tons of copper) and the Guan Yin statue (12 metres, 18 tons) at the summit are as impressive as the mountain views.

faq.heading: Questions About the 5-Day Hanoi Sapa Fansipan Package

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days. No advance application required — arrive at Noi Bai International Airport with your Malaysian passport.

items.question: How is the drive from Hanoi to Sapa?
items.answer: The drive is approximately 320 km via the Lao Cai expressway (opened 2014), taking 5–5.5 hours with comfort stops. The vehicle is a private air-conditioned van. The early 06:30 departure from Hanoi ensures arrival in Sapa before 13:00, leaving the afternoon for Cat Cat Village. The route passes through the Red River Delta flatlands for the first hour before ascending into the mountain province.

items.question: Is Fansipan physically demanding?
items.answer: No. The Sun World Fansipan Legend system means the summit is fully accessible without trekking: the Muong Hoa Hiking Train carries you from Sapa town to the cable car station, the gondola ascends to 3,143 metres, and the funicular goes the final distance to the peak. At the summit, movement is on paved paths and staircases. Normal walking fitness is sufficient. Allow 4+ hours at the summit complex to see everything.

items.question: What is the best season for Sapa?
items.answer: The rice terraces are most spectacular in September–October (golden harvest, when the paddies turn from green to gold) and May–June (water-filled reflection season, when paddies mirror the sky). The clearest mountain views and driest weather are from October to December. Winter (December–February) can be cold and misty — bring warm layers if visiting then. All seasons have their own appeal.
